flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the RandomAccess interface?
flashcards.back: It is a marker interface that indicates a List implementation supports fast random access (O(1) time complexity for index-based operations).

flashcards.front: Name two Java List implementations that implement RandomAccess.
flashcards.back: ArrayList and Vector. Both use array-based storage, enabling O(1) access time.

flashcards.front: How does RandomAccess affect algorithm performance?
flashcards.back: Algorithms like binarySearch can choose optimal implementations: index-based (fast) for RandomAccess lists or iterator-based (slower) for non-RandomAccess lists.

flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of accessing an element in a RandomAccess list?
flashcards.back: O(1). RandomAccess lists (e.g., ArrayList) allow constant-time access to any element by index.
LinkedList uses a linked node structure, requiring O(n) time to access elements by index, making it inefficient for random access operations.
